# Robotics + Embodied AI + VLA + Sim2Real Survey (2024-2026)

## 1. Vision-Language-Action (VLA) Models

**RT-1 / RT-2 (Google DeepMind, 2022/2023)**
- RT-1 (Dec 2022): Transformer trained on 130k demonstrations from 13 robots over 17 months; 97% success on 700+ training instructions. RT-2 (Jul 2023, arXiv:2307.15818): co-fine-tunes PaLI-X/PaLM-E on robot + web data, enabling chain-of-thought multi-step reasoning and emergent generalization. **Limitation:** closed weights; latency too high for dexterous control; struggles with novel skills not implied by web priors.

**OpenVLA (Stanford/Berkeley/TRI/DeepMind/PI, 2024) — arXiv:2406.09246**
- 7B-parameter open-source VLA on Llama-2 + SigLIP/DINOv2, trained on 970k Open X-Embodiment trajectories. Outperforms Diffusion Policy by 20.4% on multi-task, multi-object generalization; LoRA fine-tunable on consumer GPUs; 4-bit quantization preserves performance. **Limitation:** ~5 Hz inference; discrete action tokens limit precision; single-image observation hurts depth tasks. (https://openvla.github.io)

**π0 / π0-FAST / π0.5 (Physical Intelligence, 2024-2025)**
- π0 (Oct 2024): generalist policy combining a VLM with flow-matching action expert for high-frequency (50 Hz) continuous control across 7 robot platforms. π0-FAST (Jan 2025): autoregressive variant with DCT-based action tokenizer (5x training speedup). **π0.5** (arXiv:2504.16054, Apr 2025): adds hierarchical reasoning + heterogeneous co-training (web data, verbal instructions, multi-robot data) for open-world generalization — cleans entirely novel kitchens/bedrooms. **Limitation:** Long-horizon failures, expensive demonstration data, generalization still narrow vs. truly novel objects. (https://www.physicalintelligence.company/blog/pi05)

**Figure Helix / Figure 02 / Figure 03 (Figure AI, 2025)**
- Helix (Feb 2025): dual-process VLA — S2 (7-9 Hz internet-pretrained VLM for scene/goal reasoning) + S1 (200 Hz visuomotor policy controlling 35 DoF). Runs entirely on embedded GPU. Figure 03 (Oct 2025): redesigned hardware for Helix — 2x camera frame rate, ¼ latency, 60% wider FoV, palm cameras, 3-gram tactile fingertips; designed for 12k units/yr production. **Limitation:** closed-source; benchmark numbers not peer-reviewed; reliance on extensive in-house teleop data. (https://www.figure.ai/news/introducing-figure-03)

**NVIDIA Isaac GR00T N1 (Mar 2025) — arXiv:2503.14734**
- First open humanoid foundation model. Dual-system (System 1 fast diffusion-transformer action head + System 2 NVIDIA Eagle-2 VLM). GR00T-N1-2B (2.2B params, 1.34B VLM) samples 16 actions in 63.9 ms on L40 GPU. Trained on real trajectories + human video + synthetic data (from Cosmos/Omniverse). **Limitation:** narrow real-world dexterity benchmarks; sim2real still requires domain randomization tuning. (https://research.nvidia.com/labs/lpr/publication/gr00tn1_2025/)

**Gemini Robotics / Gemini Robotics 1.5 / -ER (Google DeepMind, 2025) — arXiv:2503.20020**
- Built on Gemini 2.0; smooth reactive manipulation across diverse embodiments and unseen environments with open-vocabulary instructions. Gemini Robotics-ER (Embodied Reasoning) and 1.5 (Sep 2025) add agentic tool use (web search planning) and on-device deployment. Powers Apptronik Apollo. **Limitation:** closed model; cloud dependency for full variants; multimodal reasoning latency.

**Magma (Microsoft, CVPR 2025) — arXiv:2502.13130**
- Foundation model unifying digital UI navigation and physical robot manipulation. Introduces Set-of-Mark (SoM) and Trace-of-Mark (ToM) annotations for action grounding. SOTA on UI + robot benchmarks with single backbone. **Limitation:** zero-shot real-robot performance still lags specialist VLAs on dexterous tasks.

## 2. Diffusion Policy & Imitation Learning

**Diffusion Policy (Chi et al., RSS 2023; IJRR 2025) — arXiv:2303.04137**
- Visuomotor policy as conditional denoising diffusion; +46.9% over prior SOTA across 12 tasks / 4 benchmarks; handles multimodal action distributions, high-dim continuous spaces, training stability via receding-horizon prediction. **Limitation:** sample-inefficient at inference (multiple denoising steps); poor cross-embodiment transfer.

**3D Diffusion Policy (DP3, 2024) — arXiv:2403.03954**
- Point-cloud encoder + diffusion policy. 85% success on 4 real tasks with only 40 demos each; +24.2% over baselines in 72 sim tasks with only 10 demos. **Limitation:** requires depth sensing; struggles with reflective/transparent objects.

**DexCap (Stanford/Movement Lab, RSS 2024)**
- Portable mocap glove + chest-mounted RGB-D for in-the-wild dexterous demonstration collection; diffusion policy on point clouds. **Limitation:** embodiment mismatch between human hand demos and robot manipulator.

**Mobile ALOHA / ALOHA (Stanford, 2024)**
- $32k bimanual whole-body teleop system; ACT (Action Chunking Transformer) imitation learning. Demonstrated cooking, cleaning. **Limitation:** ~50 demos per task; brittle to novel scenes.

**Octo (Berkeley/Stanford/CMU, RSS 2024) — arXiv:2405.12213**
- Open transformer-based diffusion policy, 800k Open X-Embodiment episodes; language- or goal-conditioned; fine-tunable to new robots/sensors in hours.

**CrossFormer (Berkeley, 2024)**
- Single transformer controlling single/dual-arm manipulators, wheeled robots, quadcopters, quadrupeds — same weights, different action heads. **Limitation:** Performance still below specialist policies per embodiment.

## 3. World Models for Robotics

**DreamerV3 (Hafner et al., Nature 2025; arXiv:2301.04104)**
- Fixed-hyperparameter MBRL across 150+ tasks (continuous/discrete, 2D/3D, sparse/dense reward). First algorithm to mine diamonds in Minecraft from scratch. **Limitation:** Sample efficiency drops in extreme partial observability; world model errors compound for very long horizons.

**Genie 1 / 2 / 3 (Google DeepMind, 2024-2025)**
- Genie 1 (Feb 2024): generates playable 2D environments from unlabeled internet video. Genie 2 (Dec 2024): action-controllable 3D environments from a single prompt image (~10s memory). **Genie 3 (Aug 2025)**: 720p, 24 fps real-time interactive worlds with multi-minute consistency. Released as "Project Genie" Jan 2026. **Limitation:** finite horizon memory; physics inconsistencies; not yet a deployed RL training environment for real robots.

**Sora / Sora 2 (OpenAI, 2024-2025)**
- "Video generation models as world simulators" thesis: emergent persistence (brush strokes, bite marks) and 3D consistency. **Limitation:** fails at brittle/shatter dynamics, cause-and-effect; no explicit dynamics control.

**V-JEPA 2 (Meta, Jun 2025) — arXiv:2506.x**
- 1.2B-param self-supervised JEPA on 1M hours of video + 62 hrs robot data. 65-80% zero-shot pick-and-place in unseen environments; ~30× faster than NVIDIA Cosmos for planning. Open-source. **Limitation:** Embedding-space prediction less direct for fine control than pixel-space; sparse robot action data.

**1X World Model (1XWM, 2024-2025)**
- Video-prediction model trained on NEO robot data to enable on-demand skill synthesis from natural language. **Limitation:** generative quality not yet matching DeepMind Genie; closed.

## 4. Sim2Real Transfer

**Domain Randomization & DrEureka (NVIDIA/UPenn/UT Austin, 2024) — arXiv:2406.01967**
- DrEureka uses LLMs to jointly synthesize reward functions and domain randomization parameters. Surpasses human-tuned baselines for quadruped yoga-ball balancing. **Limitation:** still task-specific; LLM-generated rewards can exploit simulator bugs.

**NVIDIA Isaac Lab + Isaac Sim 4.x (2024-2025)**
- GPU-accelerated framework integrating physics, photorealistic rendering, multi-sensor sim. 2M env steps/s on 8× RTX Pro 6000; >900k frames/s manipulation. Now standard for humanoid RL. (https://github.com/isaac-sim/IsaacLab)

**MuJoCo MJX + MuJoCo Playground (DeepMind, 2025) — arXiv:2502.08844**
- MJX: JAX-native, differentiable rewrite of MuJoCo on GPU/TPU. Playground deployed sim2real RL to 6 robot platforms in <8 weeks.

**Newton Physics Engine (NVIDIA + Google DeepMind + Disney, GTC 2025)**
- Open-source, GPU-accelerated, differentiable physics on NVIDIA Warp; MuJoCo + Isaac Lab compatible. 70× humanoid sim speedup, 100× for in-hand manipulation. Disney BDX droids first adopter. **Limitation:** young codebase; collision/contact accuracy still improving.

**Drake (TRI/MIT)**
- Continues as the gold standard for model-based contact-rich simulation; less used for RL than for trajectory optimization / MPC.

## 5. Humanoid Robots

**Tesla Optimus Gen 3 (Oct 2025)** — kung-fu, cooking, cleaning via observation; 5k units target end-2025; $20-30k target price.
**Figure 02/03 (2024/Oct 2025)** — see Helix above.
**Unitree H1 / G1** — open RL gym (`unitree_rl_gym`); G1 is the academic workhorse for whole-body RL.
**1X NEO** — consumer humanoid, $20k early access / $499/mo subscription; deliveries 2026.
**Apptronik Apollo** — $520M raise at $5B valuation (Feb 2026); Google DeepMind partnership using Gemini Robotics; deployed at Mercedes-Benz, GXO, Jabil.
**Sanctuary AI** — Carbon AI platform; $140M+ funding.

**Berkeley Humanoid / Fast RL on G1 (2024-2025)** — Off-policy RL learns G1 locomotion in 15 min on RTX 4090 (arXiv:2512.01996). LeVERB (Berkeley + CMU): zero-shot sim-to-real whole-body control from language.

**Whole-body teleoperation stack:**
- **HumanPlus** (Stanford, 2024) — humanoid shadowing from RGB human video.
- **H2O / OmniH2O** (CMU LeCAR, CoRL 2024) — kinematic-pose universal interface; VR + verbal + RGB control; GPT-4 autonomy; releases OmniH2O-6 dataset.
- **ASAP** (CMU/NVIDIA, 2025) — sim-pretrain motion tracking then learn a delta-action residual model from real data to close dynamics gap; enables agile parkour/dance.

**Open problems:** humanoid dexterity (bimanual contact-rich), safety verification, on-device VLA latency, reliable battery-life autonomous operation (Figure recently claimed 8-hr autonomous shifts).

## 6. Manipulation Benchmarks

- **LIBERO** (UT Austin/NVIDIA, NeurIPS 2024) — dominant VLA benchmark; lifelong learning suites (Spatial/Object/Goal/Long); recent LIBERO-PRO (arXiv:2510.03827) flags memorization issues.
- **RoboCasa** (UT Austin, RSS 2024) — arXiv:2406.02523. 100 kitchen tasks (25 atomic + 75 LLM-composed); generative-AI assets (text-to-3D, text-to-image). (https://robocasa.ai/)
- **CALVIN** (Freiburg, 2022) — long-horizon language-conditioned manipulation; still the standard for hierarchical instruction following.
- **RLBench** (Imperial, 2020) — CoppeliaSim, 100 tasks; legacy comparison.
- **ManiSkill3** (UCSD SAPIEN, 2024) — arXiv:2410.00425. GPU-parallelized rendering + physics; bridges sim-to-real for generalizable embodied AI.
- **DROID** (Stanford/Berkeley, RSS 2024) — arXiv:2403.12945. 76k demos, 564 scenes, 86 tasks across multiple universities. +22 pp in-distribution / +17 pp OOD vs. OXE.
- **Open X-Embodiment** (Google + 21 institutions, 2024) — 1M+ trajectories from 22 embodiments; foundation for OpenVLA, Octo, RT-X.

## 7. RL-Trained Manipulation Policies

- **Eureka** (NVIDIA/UPenn/UT Austin, ICLR 2024) — LLM-coded reward functions outperform human experts on 80%+ of 29 tasks; +50% avg improvement; famously taught Shadow Hand pen-spinning. (https://eureka-research.github.io)
- **DrEureka** (RSS 2024) — see above; auto sim2real reward + DR.
- **SERL** (Berkeley, 2024) — arXiv:2401.16013. Real-world RL stack (RLPD off-policy + reset-free forward-backward + impedance control). Trains peg insertion, PCB assembly, cable routing in 25-50 min, ~100% success. **Limitation:** still per-task; needs task-specific reward classifier.
- **HumanPlus / ASAP / OmniH2O** — see Section 5.

## Cross-Cutting Open Problems (2024-2026)

1. **Data scarcity** — 1M trajectories (OXE) is still <1% of what LLMs train on; teleop is the bottleneck.
2. **Latency vs. capability** — large VLMs (>3B params) give reasoning but throttle reactive control; dual-system architectures (Helix, GR00T N1, Gemini Robotics 1.5) are the consensus workaround.
3. **Generalization** — π0.5 is the first to credibly demo novel-environment cleanup; broader OOD generalization remains unsolved.
4. **Sim2real** — Newton + Isaac Lab + MJX have closed the speed gap, but contact-rich dexterity and deformables still suffer; vision domain gap mitigated by photorealistic synthetic data (Cosmos, Omniverse).
5. **Evaluation** — LIBERO-PRO shows VLAs memorize benchmarks; community pivoting toward in-the-wild evaluation suites (DROID-style).
6. **Safety / reliability** — no humanoid yet has formal safety certification for unstructured home deployment.

---

**Sources:**
- [OpenVLA arXiv 2406.09246](https://arxiv.org/abs/2406.09246) / [project site](https://openvla.github.io/)
- [π0.5 blog](https://www.physicalintelligence.company/blog/pi05) / [arXiv 2504.16054](https://arxiv.org/abs/2504.16054)
- [Figure Helix Robot Report](https://www.therobotreport.com/figure-humanoid-robots-demonstrate-helix-model-household-chores/) / [Figure 03 announcement](https://www.figure.ai/news/introducing-figure-03)
- [NVIDIA GR00T N1 newsroom](https://nvidianews.nvidia.com/news/nvidia-isaac-gr00t-n1-open-humanoid-robot-foundation-model-simulation-frameworks) / [arXiv 2503.14734](https://arxiv.org/abs/2503.14734)
- [Gemini Robotics blog](https://deepmind.google/blog/gemini-robotics-brings-ai-into-the-physical-world/) / [arXiv 2503.20020](https://arxiv.org/abs/2503.20020) / [Gemini Robotics 1.5](https://deepmind.google/blog/gemini-robotics-15-brings-ai-agents-into-the-physical-world/)
- [Magma arXiv 2502.13130](https://arxiv.org/abs/2502.13130) / [MS Research](https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/research/blog/magma-a-foundation-model-for-multimodal-ai-agents-across-digital-and-physical-worlds/)
- [Diffusion Policy arXiv 2303.04137](https://arxiv.org/abs/2303.04137) / [project](https://diffusion-policy.cs.columbia.edu/)
- [3D Diffusion Policy arXiv 2403.03954](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2403.03954) / [DexCap GitHub](https://github.com/j96w/DexCap) / [Mobile ALOHA](https://mobile-aloha.github.io/)
- [Octo arXiv 2405.12213](https://arxiv.org/abs/2405.12213) / [project](https://octo-models.github.io/)
- [DreamerV3 arXiv 2301.04104](https://arxiv.org/abs/2301.04104)
- [Genie 2 blog](https://deepmind.google/blog/genie-2-a-large-scale-foundation-world-model/) / [Genie 3 blog](https://deepmind.google/blog/genie-3-a-new-frontier-for-world-models/)
- [V-JEPA 2 blog](https://ai.meta.com/blog/v-jepa-2-world-model-benchmarks/) / [Robot Report](https://www.therobotreport.com/meta-v-jepa-2-world-model-uses-raw-video-to-train-robots/)

aiTitle: Survey of deep reinforcement learning and agentic AI

content.text: 繼續寫 Part 2 (Systems & Platforms)。

input.content:
# Part 2 — Survey of DRL Systems and Platforms

DRL 的進步從來不是只有演算法。一個能在 **單一 GPU 上每秒模擬數萬個機器人** 的 simulator、一個能在 **百萬時間步** 內收斂的 distributed trainer，往往才是論文背後真正的決勝點。本章節整理十個現代 DRL 系統，沿四個維度做比較：**系統架構 / 模擬環境 / 支援演算法 / 目標應用**，並指出每個系統的優勢與限制。

## 2.1 NVIDIA Isaac Gym / Isaac Sim / Isaac Lab

**架構。** Isaac Gym（2021 釋出）是 **GPU-end-to-end** 的機器人模擬器——physics simulation、observation rendering 與 RL training 全部在 GPU 上，不必走 CPU↔GPU 來回。一張 RTX 4090 可同時模擬數千到上萬個並行環境（envs），把 PPO 從「兩週」壓到「數小時」收斂。2023 年起 NVIDIA 推出 **Isaac Sim**（基於 Omniverse 的 photorealistic simulator）與 **Isaac Lab**（取代舊版 Isaac Gym 的學術用 RL 框架）；2024 年又跟 Google DeepMind、Disney 聯手發布 **Newton**——一個專為 robotics 設計的下一代 differentiable physics engine（建立在 NVIDIA Warp 上）。

**支援演算法。** PPO、SAC、TRPO（透過 rl_games / skrl / Stable-Baselines3 整合）；2024–2025 起 NVIDIA 大力推 **Eureka / DrEureka**（用 LLM 自動生成 reward function）與 **GR00T**（humanoid foundation model）。

**應用。** 四足機器人（ANYmal / Spot）、人形機器人（GR00T training, 1X NEO）、機械手（Allegro / Shadow Hand）、Sim2Real 的所有環節。

**優勢。** 速度無人能敵；photorealistic rendering；NVIDIA 的 GPU/CUDA 生態。**限制。** 對非 NVIDIA GPU 不友善；deformable / fluid 支援仍弱；學習曲線陡（Omniverse 的 USD 格式並不友善）。

## 2.2 Habitat Lab (Meta AI)

**架構。** Meta AI 開發的 **embodied AI** 模擬平台。Habitat-Sim 用 C++ + Bullet/Magnum 做 high-throughput rendering（10000+ FPS in headless mode），Habitat-Lab 是 Python 端的 RL/IL framework。2023 年釋出 **Habitat 3.0** 支援 humanoid avatar 和 human-robot interaction；2024 年 **Habitat-Matterport 3D Semantics (HM3D)** 提供 1000+ 棟真實住宅的 photorealistic 3D 掃描。

**支援演算法。** PPO、DD-PPO（distributed PPO）、imitation learning。

**應用。** Visual navigation（PointNav, ObjectNav, ImageNav）、language-guided navigation、mobile manipulation、social navigation。

**優勢。** **室內具身導航的事實標準**；rendering 與物理速度極快；資料集生態完整（MP3D、HM3D、Gibson、ReplicaCAD）。**限制。** 物理模擬不像 Isaac 那麼精準（足以做 navigation 但 manipulation 較弱）；以 Linux 為主。

## 2.3 CARLA

**架構。** 開源的都市自駕模擬器（Intel + CVC + Toyota Research），基於 Unreal Engine 4/5。提供完整的城市環境（CARLA Towns）、感測器（RGB、Depth、Lidar、Radar、IMU、GNSS）、可調節天氣、行人/車輛 NPC，以及 Python/C++ API。CARLA Leaderboard 是業界公認的端到端自駕 benchmark。

**支援演算法。** 任意 DRL 演算法（環境用 Gym/Gymnasium 包好即可）；近年大量出現基於 model-based RL（如 Latent World Models）與 imitation learning + RL hybrid 的工作。

**應用。** 自駕策略 / planning / control；多 agent driving；強化學習與規則式 planner 的對比。

**優勢。** **唯一被產業界廣泛採用的開源自駕模擬器**；社群活躍；CARLA Challenge 比賽提供共同 leaderboard。**限制。** Unreal 端 rendering 很重，難以同時跑大量 envs；sim2real gap 仍然大；CARLA scenarios 的 distribution 與真實道路不完全重疊。

## 2.4 Unity ML-Agents

**架構。** Unity 推出的開源 toolkit，讓任何 Unity 場景變成 Gym-compatible RL environment。架構分三層：**Unity scene + ML-Agents C# Behavior** → **Python low-level API (gRPC)** → **mlagents-learn trainer**（內建 PPO、SAC、POCA for multi-agent）。

**應用。** **遊戲 AI 訓練**（NPC 行為、難度調節）、教育示範、small-scale robotics（藉由 Unity Robotics Hub 整合 ROS）。

**優勢。** 對遊戲開發者最友善；3D 物件 / 動畫資產庫巨大；跨平台部署。**限制。** 物理模擬不及 Isaac/MuJoCo；trainer 不及 RLlib 強大；不適合大規模 distributed training。

## 2.5 Ray RLlib

**架構。** UC Berkeley RISELab → Anyscale 商業化。建構在 **Ray** distributed computing 上，提供 **rollout workers / learner workers / replay buffer** 抽象，能輕鬆從單機擴到上千 CPU/GPU。內建超過 30 種演算法的官方實作（PPO, IMPALA, APPO, SAC, DQN, DDPG, MARL 等）。

**應用。** 產業界的大規模 DRL 訓練；推薦系統、game AI、廣告 bidding；Anyscale 是目前最完整的 commercial DRL platform。

**優勢。** **scalability 一流**；演算法選擇齊全；產業級維護。**限制。** 抽象多、API 學習曲線陡；2.x → 3.x 大改動讓社群一度抱怨穩定性；單機快速實驗反而不如 SB3 / CleanRL 順手。

## 2.6 Stable-Baselines3 (SB3)

**架構。** PyTorch 重寫的 Stable-Baselines（OpenAI Baselines 的 fork）。設計哲學是 **single-machine、可讀性優先、reproducibility 優先**。提供 PPO、A2C、DQN、SAC、TD3、DDPG 等核心演算法，所有 baseline benchmark 與 hyperparameter 公開在 **rl-baselines3-zoo**。

**應用。** 研究 baseline、教學、小型產業 prototyping。

**優勢。** **學術界最被引用的 PyTorch DRL 庫**；文件完整；rl-zoo 提供了 100+ 個調好的 hyperparameter set。**限制。** 不支援 distributed training（單 GPU + 多 env 上限）；演算法保守（不追新 SOTA 如 DreamerV3）。

## 2.7 FinRL (AI4Finance Foundation)

**架構。** 哥倫比亞大學 Xiao-Yang Liu 團隊主導的開源金融 DRL 框架。三層架構：**FinRL-Meta**（金融資料 + 環境）→ **FinRL** （演算法層，基於 SB3 / ElegantRL / RLlib）→ **FinRL-Tutorials**（範例）。2024 年釋出 **FinRL-DeepSeek** 結合 LLM signal 與 DRL trader。

**應用。** 股票 / 加密貨幣 / 期貨的演算法交易、portfolio optimization、market making、order execution。

**優勢。** **唯一被廣泛採用的金融專用 DRL 框架**；提供 trading environments、tutorials、leaderboard；活躍維護。**限制。** 真實金融市場的 non-stationarity 讓 sim-trained policy 難以泛化；論文中的 outperformance 在實盤常打折扣。

## 2.8 MineDojo (NVIDIA + 學術界)

**架構。** Fan et al., NeurIPS 2022 [^fan2022minedojo]。在 Minecraft 之上建造的 open-ended embodied agent benchmark。三大資產：
1. **Simulator**（基於 MineRL，提供 thousands of 任務）；
2. **資料集**：730k YouTube 影片 + 7000+ Wiki 頁 + 340k Reddit posts；
3. **基礎模型 MineCLIP**：對齊 Minecraft 影片與文字描述。

**應用。** Voyager (Wang et al., NeurIPS 2023 [^wang2023voyager]) 在此上面驗證 LLM-driven lifelong learning；後續 GITM、JARVIS-1 等基於 MineDojo 做評估。

**優勢。** **目前唯一規模化的開放世界 embodied benchmark**；任務多樣性 + 海量 unlabeled video；極適合驗證 foundation agents。**限制。** Minecraft 本身的視覺/物理與真實世界差距大；setup 複雜（需要 Java + Mod 環境）。

## 2.9 OpenVLA (Stanford + Google DeepMind + Toyota Research, 2024)

**架構。** Kim et al., 2024 [^kim2024openvla]。**開源 Vision-Language-Action foundation model**，7B 參數，在 Open X-Embodiment 970k+ robot trajectory 上微調 Llama 2 + DINOv2/SigLIP visual encoder。輸出是 tokenized 7-DoF action sequence。

**支援演算法。** Primarily imitation learning + behavior cloning；可用 RL fine-tune（後續工作如 RLDP、PARL）。

**應用。** Multi-embodiment manipulation; benchmark 在 BridgeData-V2、LIBERO 等。

**優勢。** **首個 open-weights VLA**，打破 RT-2 的閉源壟斷；參數量適中（消費級 GPU 可微調）。**限制。** 在新 embodiment 上仍需 demonstration fine-tuning；推論 latency 對 high-frequency control 是挑戰；缺乏線上 RL 機制。

## 2.10 Microsoft AirSim / Project AirSim

**架構。** Microsoft Research 的開源無人機/車輛模擬器，建構在 Unreal Engine 上。提供 photorealistic environments、physics-based flight dynamics 與 multiple sensor modalities。**注意**：原 AirSim repo 已 archived（2022 年底），Microsoft 把後續開發轉到商業化的 **Project AirSim**（基於 Azure cloud）。社群 fork **Cosys-AirSim** 繼續維護開源版。

**應用。** UAV 自主飛行訓練、深度視覺 + RL 控制、CV pipeline 測試。

**優勢。** **學術界 UAV RL 的主要實驗平台**；豐富感測器；Multi-vehicle 支援。**限制。** 已停止官方開源維護是隱患；對沒用過 Unreal 的人 setup 不容易；rendering throughput 限制大規模並行訓練。

---

## 2.11 比較表

\begin{longtable}{p{2.8cm}p{3.6cm}p{3.2cm}p{3.6cm}}
\toprule
\textbf{平台} & \textbf{主要 simulator / 環境} & \textbf{支援演算法} & \textbf{目標應用} \\
\midrule
Isaac Lab / Sim & GPU end-to-end physics + photorealistic & PPO/SAC + Eureka, GR00T & Humanoid / 機械手 / quadruped \\
Habitat 3.0 & Photoreal 室內 3D 掃描 (HM3D) & PPO / DD-PPO / IL & 室內具身導航 + HRI \\
CARLA & Unreal-based 城市駕駛 & 任意 DRL (Gym API) + IL & 自駕、planning \\
Unity ML-Agents & 任意 Unity 場景 & PPO / SAC / POCA & 遊戲 AI、教學 \\
RLlib & 任意 Gym 環境 (Ray cluster) & 30+ 演算法 (含 MARL) & 大規模產業 DRL \\
Stable-Baselines3 & 任意 Gym 環境 (單機) & PPO / SAC / TD3 / DQN ... & 研究 baseline、教學 \\
FinRL & 金融資料 (股票/加密) & 透過 SB3/RLlib/ElegantRL & 演算法交易、portfolio \\
MineDojo & Minecraft + 多模態資料 & 任意 + LLM agent & 開放世界 / 終身學習 \\
OpenVLA & Open X-Embodiment 資料集 & IL（可 RL fine-tune） & 多 embodiment manipulation \\
AirSim & Unreal 無人機 / 車輛 & 任意 DRL (Gym API) & UAV、autonomous driving \\
\bottomrule
\end{longtable}

---

[^fan2022minedojo]: Fan, L. et al. "MineDojo: Building open-ended embodied agents with internet-scale knowledge." *NeurIPS Datasets and Benchmarks Track* (2022).
[^wang2023voyager]: Wang, G. et al. "Voyager: An open-ended embodied agent with large language models." *arXiv:2305.16291* (2023).
[^kim2024openvla]: Kim, M. J. et al. "OpenVLA: An open-source vision-language-action model." *arXiv:2406.09246* (2024).

toolUseResult.content:
# Part 2 — Survey of DRL Systems and Platforms

DRL 的進步從來不是只有演算法。一個能在 **單一 GPU 上每秒模擬數萬個機器人** 的 simulator、一個能在 **百萬時間步** 內收斂的 distributed trainer，往往才是論文背後真正的決勝點。本章節整理十個現代 DRL 系統，沿四個維度做比較：**系統架構 / 模擬環境 / 支援演算法 / 目標應用**，並指出每個系統的優勢與限制。

## 2.1 NVIDIA Isaac Gym / Isaac Sim / Isaac Lab

**架構。** Isaac Gym（2021 釋出）是 **GPU-end-to-end** 的機器人模擬器——physics simulation、observation rendering 與 RL training 全部在 GPU 上，不必走 CPU↔GPU 來回。一張 RTX 4090 可同時模擬數千到上萬個並行環境（envs），把 PPO 從「兩週」壓到「數小時」收斂。2023 年起 NVIDIA 推出 **Isaac Sim**（基於 Omniverse 的 photorealistic simulator）與 **Isaac Lab**（取代舊版 Isaac Gym 的學術用 RL 框架）；2024 年又跟 Google DeepMind、Disney 聯手發布 **Newton**——一個專為 robotics 設計的下一代 differentiable physics engine（建立在 NVIDIA Warp 上）。

**支援演算法。** PPO、SAC、TRPO（透過 rl_games / skrl / Stable-Baselines3 整合）；2024–2025 起 NVIDIA 大力推 **Eureka / DrEureka**（用 LLM 自動生成 reward function）與 **GR00T**（humanoid foundation model）。

**應用。** 四足機器人（ANYmal / Spot）、人形機器人（GR00T training, 1X NEO）、機械手（Allegro / Shadow Hand）、Sim2Real 的所有環節。

**優勢。** 速度無人能敵；photorealistic rendering；NVIDIA 的 GPU/CUDA 生態。**限制。** 對非 NVIDIA GPU 不友善；deformable / fluid 支援仍弱；學習曲線陡（Omniverse 的 USD 格式並不友善）。

## 2.2 Habitat Lab (Meta AI)

**架構。** Meta AI 開發的 **embodied AI** 模擬平台。Habitat-Sim 用 C++ + Bullet/Magnum 做 high-throughput rendering（10000+ FPS in headless mode），Habitat-Lab 是 Python 端的 RL/IL framework。2023 年釋出 **Habitat 3.0** 支援 humanoid avatar 和 human-robot interaction；2024 年 **Habitat-Matterport 3D Semantics (HM3D)** 提供 1000+ 棟真實住宅的 photorealistic 3D 掃描。

**支援演算法。** PPO、DD-PPO（distributed PPO）、imitation learning。

**應用。** Visual navigation（PointNav, ObjectNav, ImageNav）、language-guided navigation、mobile manipulation、social navigation。

**優勢。** **室內具身導航的事實標準**；rendering 與物理速度極快；資料集生態完整（MP3D、HM3D、Gibson、ReplicaCAD）。**限制。** 物理模擬不像 Isaac 那麼精準（足以做 navigation 但 manipulation 較弱）；以 Linux 為主。

## 2.3 CARLA

**架構。** 開源的都市自駕模擬器（Intel + CVC + Toyota Research），基於 Unreal Engine 4/5。提供完整的城市環境（CARLA Towns）、感測器（RGB、Depth、Lidar、Radar、IMU、GNSS）、可調節天氣、行人/車輛 NPC，以及 Python/C++ API。CARLA Leaderboard 是業界公認的端到端自駕 benchmark。

**支援演算法。** 任意 DRL 演算法（環境用 Gym/Gymnasium 包好即可）；近年大量出現基於 model-based RL（如 Latent World Models）與 imitation learning + RL hybrid 的工作。

**應用。** 自駕策略 / planning / control；多 agent driving；強化學習與規則式 planner 的對比。

**優勢。** **唯一被產業界廣泛採用的開源自駕模擬器**；社群活躍；CARLA Challenge 比賽提供共同 leaderboard。**限制。** Unreal 端 rendering 很重，難以同時跑大量 envs；sim2real gap 仍然大；CARLA scenarios 的 distribution 與真實道路不完全重疊。

## 2.4 Unity ML-Agents

**架構。** Unity 推出的開源 toolkit，讓任何 Unity 場景變成 Gym-compatible RL environment。架構分三層：**Unity scene + ML-Agents C# Behavior** → **Python low-level API (gRPC)** → **mlagents-learn trainer**（內建 PPO、SAC、POCA for multi-agent）。

**應用。** **遊戲 AI 訓練**（NPC 行為、難度調節）、教育示範、small-scale robotics（藉由 Unity Robotics Hub 整合 ROS）。

**優勢。** 對遊戲開發者最友善；3D 物件 / 動畫資產庫巨大；跨平台部署。**限制。** 物理模擬不及 Isaac/MuJoCo；trainer 不及 RLlib 強大；不適合大規模 distributed training。

## 2.5 Ray RLlib

**架構。** UC Berkeley RISELab → Anyscale 商業化。建構在 **Ray** distributed computing 上，提供 **rollout workers / learner workers / replay buffer** 抽象，能輕鬆從單機擴到上千 CPU/GPU。內建超過 30 種演算法的官方實作（PPO, IMPALA, APPO, SAC, DQN, DDPG, MARL 等）。

**應用。** 產業界的大規模 DRL 訓練；推薦系統、game AI、廣告 bidding；Anyscale 是目前最完整的 commercial DRL platform。

**優勢。** **scalability 一流**；演算法選擇齊全；產業級維護。**限制。** 抽象多、API 學習曲線陡；2.x → 3.x 大改動讓社群一度抱怨穩定性；單機快速實驗反而不如 SB3 / CleanRL 順手。

## 2.6 Stable-Baselines3 (SB3)

**架構。** PyTorch 重寫的 Stable-Baselines（OpenAI Baselines 的 fork）。設計哲學是 **single-machine、可讀性優先、reproducibility 優先**。提供 PPO、A2C、DQN、SAC、TD3、DDPG 等核心演算法，所有 baseline benchmark 與 hyperparameter 公開在 **rl-baselines3-zoo**。

**應用。** 研究 baseline、教學、小型產業 prototyping。

**優勢。** **學術界最被引用的 PyTorch DRL 庫**；文件完整；rl-zoo 提供了 100+ 個調好的 hyperparameter set。**限制。** 不支援 distributed training（單 GPU + 多 env 上限）；演算法保守（不追新 SOTA 如 DreamerV3）。

## 2.7 FinRL (AI4Finance Foundation)

**架構。** 哥倫比亞大學 Xiao-Yang Liu 團隊主導的開源金融 DRL 框架。三層架構：**FinRL-Meta**（金融資料 + 環境）→ **FinRL** （演算法層，基於 SB3 / ElegantRL / RLlib）→ **FinRL-Tutorials**（範例）。2024 年釋出 **FinRL-DeepSeek** 結合 LLM signal 與 DRL trader。

**應用。** 股票 / 加密貨幣 / 期貨的演算法交易、portfolio optimization、market making、order execution。

**優勢。** **唯一被廣泛採用的金融專用 DRL 框架**；提供 trading environments、tutorials、leaderboard；活躍維護。**限制。** 真實金融市場的 non-stationarity 讓 sim-trained policy 難以泛化；論文中的 outperformance 在實盤常打折扣。

## 2.8 MineDojo (NVIDIA + 學術界)

**架構。** Fan et al., NeurIPS 2022 [^fan2022minedojo]。在 Minecraft 之上建造的 open-ended embodied agent benchmark。三大資產：
1. **Simulator**（基於 MineRL，提供 thousands of 任務）；
2. **資料集**：730k YouTube 影片 + 7000+ Wiki 頁 + 340k Reddit posts；
3. **基礎模型 MineCLIP**：對齊 Minecraft 影片與文字描述。

**應用。** Voyager (Wang et al., NeurIPS 2023 [^wang2023voyager]) 在此上面驗證 LLM-driven lifelong learning；後續 GITM、JARVIS-1 等基於 MineDojo 做評估。

**優勢。** **目前唯一規模化的開放世界 embodied benchmark**；任務多樣性 + 海量 unlabeled video；極適合驗證 foundation agents。**限制。** Minecraft 本身的視覺/物理與真實世界差距大；setup 複雜（需要 Java + Mod 環境）。

## 2.9 OpenVLA (Stanford + Google DeepMind + Toyota Research, 2024)

**架構。** Kim et al., 2024 [^kim2024openvla]。**開源 Vision-Language-Action foundation model**，7B 參數，在 Open X-Embodiment 970k+ robot trajectory 上微調 Llama 2 + DINOv2/SigLIP visual encoder。輸出是 tokenized 7-DoF action sequence。

**支援演算法。** Primarily imitation learning + behavior cloning；可用 RL fine-tune（後續工作如 RLDP、PARL）。

**應用。** Multi-embodiment manipulation; benchmark 在 BridgeData-V2、LIBERO 等。

**優勢。** **首個 open-weights VLA**，打破 RT-2 的閉源壟斷；參數量適中（消費級 GPU 可微調）。**限制。** 在新 embodiment 上仍需 demonstration fine-tuning；推論 latency 對 high-frequency control 是挑戰；缺乏線上 RL 機制。

## 2.10 Microsoft AirSim / Project AirSim

**架構。** Microsoft Research 的開源無人機/車輛模擬器，建構在 Unreal Engine 上。提供 photorealistic environments、physics-based flight dynamics 與 multiple sensor modalities。**注意**：原 AirSim repo 已 archived（2022 年底），Microsoft 把後續開發轉到商業化的 **Project AirSim**（基於 Azure cloud）。社群 fork **Cosys-AirSim** 繼續維護開源版。

**應用。** UAV 自主飛行訓練、深度視覺 + RL 控制、CV pipeline 測試。

**優勢。** **學術界 UAV RL 的主要實驗平台**；豐富感測器；Multi-vehicle 支援。**限制。** 已停止官方開源維護是隱患；對沒用過 Unreal 的人 setup 不容易；rendering throughput 限制大規模並行訓練。

---

## 2.11 比較表

\begin{longtable}{p{2.8cm}p{3.6cm}p{3.2cm}p{3.6cm}}
\toprule
\textbf{平台} & \textbf{主要 simulator / 環境} & \textbf{支援演算法} & \textbf{目標應用} \\
\midrule
Isaac Lab / Sim & GPU end-to-end physics + photorealistic & PPO/SAC + Eureka, GR00T & Humanoid / 機械手 / quadruped \\
Habitat 3.0 & Photoreal 室內 3D 掃描 (HM3D) & PPO / DD-PPO / IL & 室內具身導航 + HRI \\
CARLA & Unreal-based 城市駕駛 & 任意 DRL (Gym API) + IL & 自駕、planning \\
Unity ML-Agents & 任意 Unity 場景 & PPO / SAC / POCA & 遊戲 AI、教學 \\
RLlib & 任意 Gym 環境 (Ray cluster) & 30+ 演算法 (含 MARL) & 大規模產業 DRL \\
Stable-Baselines3 & 任意 Gym 環境 (單機) & PPO / SAC / TD3 / DQN ... & 研究 baseline、教學 \\
FinRL & 金融資料 (股票/加密) & 透過 SB3/RLlib/ElegantRL & 演算法交易、portfolio \\
MineDojo & Minecraft + 多模態資料 & 任意 + LLM agent & 開放世界 / 終身學習 \\
OpenVLA & Open X-Embodiment 資料集 & IL（可 RL fine-tune） & 多 embodiment manipulation \\
AirSim & Unreal 無人機 / 車輛 & 任意 DRL (Gym API) & UAV、autonomous driving \\
\bottomrule
\end{longtable}

---

[^fan2022minedojo]: Fan, L. et al. "MineDojo: Building open-ended embodied agents with internet-scale knowledge." *NeurIPS Datasets and Benchmarks Track* (2022).
[^wang2023voyager]: Wang, G. et al. "Voyager: An open-ended embodied agent with large language models." *arXiv:2305.16291* (2023).
[^kim2024openvla]: Kim, M. J. et al. "OpenVLA: An open-source vision-language-action model." *arXiv:2406.09246* (2024).

# Agentic AI / LLM Agents / RLHF / RLAIF — Literature Survey Notes (HW4)

## 1. LLM Agents (2024–2026)

### OpenAI's Agent Stack
- **Computer-Using Agent (CUA) / Operator** (Jan 2025): GPT-4o vision + RL-trained CUA model that performs GUI manipulation (typing, clicking, scrolling) in a browser; upgraded to o3-based version in 2025. Pixel-level perception with self-correction loop and hand-back-to-user on uncertainty. https://openai.com/index/computer-using-agent/
- **Deep Research** (Feb 2025): o3-based multi-step research agent that browses, synthesizes, and writes long reports; trained with RL on research-task verifiers. https://openai.com/index/introducing-deep-research/
- **GPT-5 / 5.2 / 5.5** (Aug–Dec 2025): "Steerable, collaborative" agentic mode; 74.9% SWE-bench Verified, 88% Aider Polyglot; GPT-5.5 hits 82.7% on agentic coding bench, designed for multi-tool coordination with explainable pre-tool reasoning. https://openai.com/index/introducing-gpt-5/
- **Open problem**: Reward hacking on benchmarks (UC Berkeley showed all 8 major agent benchmarks crackable, Apr 2026); reliability of long-horizon trajectories.

### Anthropic Agent Stack
- **Claude Computer Use** (Oct 2024): Screenshot-based GUI control via tool API. Documented in CUA tool spec.
- **Model Context Protocol (MCP)** (Nov 2024): Open JSON-RPC 2.0 standard (LSP-inspired) for connecting LLMs to tools/data sources. By Dec 2025: 97M monthly SDK downloads; donated to the Linux Foundation's Agentic AI Foundation co-founded with Block and OpenAI. https://www.anthropic.com/news/model-context-protocol
- **Claude Code** (2024–2026): Terminal-resident agentic coding tool. Single-threaded master loop with permission system, tool layer (Read/Glob/Grep/Edit/Bash), regex search over vector DB. Newer multi-agent variant decomposes planner/executor/debugger/validator. https://www.anthropic.com/product/claude-code ; arXiv:2604.14228
- **Claude Skills** (2025): Packaged capability bundles invokable by name.
- **Open problem**: Permission UX vs. autonomy trade-off; cross-session memory.

### Google Agent Stack
- **Project Astra** (Dec 2024 / I/O 2025): Multimodal "universal AI assistant" with cross-device persistent context (~10 min session memory) on phones and prototype glasses.
- **Project Mariner** (Dec 2024 / I/O 2025): Gemini 2.5 Pro-powered web agent that runs up to 10 parallel tasks; integrated into AI Mode for reservations/bookings.
- **Gemini 2.5 Pro / Deep Think** (I/O 2025): Extended into "world model" for planning and imagined experience simulation. https://blog.google/technology/google-deepmind/google-gemini-ai-update-december-2024/

### Frameworks (orchestration)
- **LangGraph** (v1.0, late 2025): Stateful graph with conditional edges; now default LangChain runtime; production-grade auditability. https://www.langchain.com/langgraph
- **CrewAI**: Role-based "crews"; $18M Series A, ~100k agent executions/day, claimed 60% F500 use.
- **AutoGen → Microsoft Agent Framework** (Oct 2025 merger with Semantic Kernel; GA Q1 2026): GroupChat conversational pattern; ICLR 2024 LLM-Agents workshop paper (Wu et al.). http://ryenwhite.com/papers/WuLLMAgents2024.pdf
- **AutoGPT / BabyAGI lineage**: Largely superseded by graph-based frameworks; survived as conceptual precursors.

### Embodied / open-ended agents
- **Voyager** (Wang et al., NeurIPS 2023): GPT-4 Minecraft agent with (1) automatic curriculum, (2) growing skill library of executable JavaScript code, (3) iterative prompting with env feedback + self-verification. Code-as-action; bypasses fine-tuning. arXiv:2305.16291
- **SOTA limitation**: Skill library bloat; reliance on closed-source GPT-4; brittle to perception shifts.

---

## 2. RLHF / RLAIF

### Direct Alignment Methods
- **DPO** (Rafailov et al., NeurIPS 2023): Closed-form mapping from reward to policy lets pairwise preference loss train policy directly — no reward model, no online sampling. Matches/exceeds PPO-RLHF on sentiment and summarization. arXiv:2305.18290
- **IPO** (Azar et al., AISTATS 2024): Replaces Bradley-Terry with bounded objective; fixes DPO's unbounded margin divergence on deterministic preferences. Better OOD generalization.
- **KTO** (Ethayarajh et al., ICML 2024 spotlight): Prospect-theory utility maximization using only binary "good/bad" labels — no pairs needed. Matches DPO at scale; far cheaper labeling. arXiv:2402.01306
- **Open problem**: Reward hacking, length bias, preference inversion under distribution shift; unified theory still being developed (e.g., arXiv:2601.06108).

### Constitutional AI / RLAIF
- **Constitutional AI** (Bai et al., Anthropic, 2022; arXiv:2212.08073): Two phases — (1) SL: self-critique + revise using a written "constitution"; (2) RL: AI-generated preference labels train a preference model used for RLHF — coined "RLAIF". Yields non-evasive harmless assistants.
- **RLAIF vs RLHF** (Lee et al., Google, ICML 2024; arXiv:2309.00267): Across summarization, helpful + harmless dialogue, RLAIF ≈ RLHF when judged head-to-head; AI labeler can match policy size. d-RLAIF (direct rewards from off-the-shelf LLM, no RM) actually beats canonical RLAIF.
- **Open problem**: Sycophancy from same-family labelers; constitutional principles brittle under adversarial prompts ("Constitution or Collapse?" arXiv:2504.04918).

### Reasoning RL (PRM/ORM/RLVR)
- **Let's Verify Step by Step** (Lightman et al., OpenAI, ICLR 2024; arXiv:2305.20050): PRMs (step-level feedback) beat ORMs on MATH; releases PRM800K (800k step labels on 75k solutions); 78% pass on MATH subset via PRM-based reranking.
- **DeepSeek-R1** (Guo et al., Nature 2025 — first peer-reviewed LLM in Nature): Pure RL with verifiable rewards (math = symbolic check, code = unit-test execution) using **GRPO** (Group Relative Policy Optimization) — drops the value critic, normalizes within a group of K samples; ~147K H800 GPU-hrs (~10× cheaper than o1-class). Matches o1-level reasoning.
- **OpenAI o1 / o3** (Sep 2024 / 2025): RL-trained long-CoT reasoning; introduced inference-time scaling law (perf ↑ with both train-time RL and test-time thinking budget). Open-source replications: QwQ, DeepSeek-R1, LIMO, s1 (arXiv:2501.19393).
- **RLVR theory**: "RLVR implicitly incentivizes correct reasoning in base LLMs" (arXiv:2506.14245) — extends reasoning boundary, not merely sampling efficiency. New CoT-Pass@K metric.
- **Open problem**: Reward over-optimization on narrow verifiers; limited transfer of math/code RL to open-ended reasoning; "Crossing the Reward Bridge" (arXiv:2503.23829) tries non-verifiable domains.

---

## 3. Tool-using / Function-calling Agents

- **ReAct** (Yao et al., ICLR 2023): Interleaved Reason + Act traces; baseline for almost all later agent loops.
- **Toolformer** (Schick et al., NeurIPS 2023): Self-supervised insertion of API call tokens during pretraining.
- **Gorilla** (Patil et al., NeurIPS 2024): Fine-tuned LLaMA for API selection across 1.6k+ APIs; spawned Berkeley Function Calling Leaderboard (BFCL v4 — agentic, multi-hop, error recovery). https://github.com/ShishirPatil/gorilla
- **ToolACE** (ICLR 2025): Synthetic tool-use data pipeline, SOTA on BFCL.

### Benchmarks
- **SWE-bench / SWE-bench Verified** (Jimenez et al., ICLR 2024): 2,294 real GitHub issues, execution-based; current SOTA ~75–82% (GPT-5/5.5). Note: OpenAI ceased reporting Verified after eval-set leakage.
- **GAIA** (Mialon et al., Meta, ICLR 2024): Compound tool-use questions with single ground truths.
- **AgentBench** (Liu et al., ICLR 2024): 8 environments (OS, DB, KG, card game, web shopping, etc.).
- **WebArena** (Zhou et al., ICLR 2024): 812 long-horizon web tasks; baseline 14.4% → 61.7% (IBM CUGA early 2025).
- **OSWorld** (Xie et al., NeurIPS 2024): 369 cross-app real-OS tasks; ~60 pt human-AI gap at launch.
- **τ-bench** (Sierra/Yao, 2024): Tests *reliability* (pass^k) across multiple runs — surfaces consistency problems hidden by one-shot benchmarks. https://benchmarkingagents.com/agent-benchmarks/
- **Open problem**: Reward hacking — UC Berkeley CRDI (Apr 2026) broke all 8 major agent benchmarks via automated reward-hacking agent.

---

## 4. Multi-Agent Collaboration

- **Generative Agents** (Park et al., Stanford, UIST 2023; arXiv:2304.03442): 25 simulacra in a sandbox town with reflect/plan/observe memory stream; produced emergent social behaviors (party planning, gossip).
- **CAMEL** (Li et al., NeurIPS 2023; arXiv:2303.17760): "Inception prompting" for role-playing pairs (user/assistant); generated AI Society + Code datasets; foundation of the CAMEL-AI OSS ecosystem.
- **MetaGPT** (Hong et al., ICLR 2024 oral; arXiv:2308.00352): SOPs assigned to agent roles (PM, architect, engineer, QA); 85.9 / 87.7 Pass@1 on code benchmarks at release.
- **ChatDev** (Qian et al., ACL 2024; arXiv:2307.07924): Waterfall software co. with CEO/CTO/programmer/tester chat roles.
- **AutoGen** (Wu et al., ICLR 2024 workshop / COLM 2024): More flexible GroupChat with broader scenarios than MetaGPT/ChatDev.

### MARL (classical RL)
- 2024 surveys (e.g., Sci. Direct S2949855424000042, arXiv:2412.21088): Cooperative MARL still dominated by CTDE family (QMIX/MAPPO); open challenges: curse of dimensionality, non-stationarity, multi-task scalability across heterogeneous reward/state/action spaces.
- Convergence with LLM agents is recent and underexplored — LLM-based agents typically lack the credit-assignment formalism of MARL.

---

## 5. Foundation Agents / Self-improving

- **SIMA** (DeepMind, Mar 2024; arXiv:2404.10179): Keyboard/mouse generalist trained across many commercial 3D games via language instructions; 31% human-relative success at launch.
- **SIMA 2** (Nov 2025; arXiv:2512.04797): Gemini-powered; ~2× v1 success; near-human in trained envs; transfer to unseen games. https://deepmind.google/blog/sima-2-an-agent-that-plays-reasons-and-learns-with-you-in-virtual-3d-worlds/
- **Magma** (Microsoft, CVPR 2025; arXiv:2502.13130): VLA foundation model for digital + physical action. Uses Set-of-Mark (SoM) for action grounding and Trace-of-Mark (ToM) for action planning; SOTA on UI navigation and robot manipulation. https://microsoft.github.io/Magma/
- **STaR** (Zelikman et al., NeurIPS 2022; arXiv:2203.14465): Bootstrap rationales — generate, filter on correct answers, fine-tune; precursor to all "rationale RL" work.
- **Self-Rewarding LMs** (Yuan et al., Meta/NYU, ICML 2024; arXiv:2401.10020): Model serves as both generator and LLM-as-judge over iterative DPO rounds; both instruction-following AND reward-giving improve.
- **Meta-Rewarding LMs** (Wu et al., arXiv:2407.19594): Extends with a meta-judge layer to reduce judge bias.
- **Open problem**: Reward-model collapse over many self-iterations; lack of grounding when no external verifier exists.

---

## 6. Memory Systems

- **Long context (1M+ tokens)**: Gemini 1.5/2.5, Claude 200k–1M; cheap but expensive at inference and lossy in needle-in-haystack at long depths.
- **RAG**: Standard external-knowledge baseline; struggles with multi-hop and temporal updates.
- **MemGPT** (Packer et al., 2023; arXiv:2310.08560; commercialized as Letta): OS-style hierarchical memory — core (RAM), recall + archival (disk), with LLM-callable paging functions. Evaluated on document QA beyond context and multi-session chat. https://research.memgpt.ai/
- **Generative-agent memory** (Park et al. 2023): Memory stream + recency/importance/relevance retrieval + reflection consolidation; foundational design pattern reused in LangGraph state stores and Astra session memory.
- **Open problem**: No standard benchmark for episodic vs. semantic memory; trade-off between context-window scaling laws and structured retrieval is unresolved; forgetting/consolidation policy.

---

## Cross-cutting open problems for HW4 framing
1. **Reliability vs. capability gap** (τ-bench, OSWorld) — agents pass single shots but fail multi-attempt consistency.
2. **Benchmark gaming / reward hacking** — UC Berkeley CRDI 2026 result threatens entire agent-eval edifice.
3. **RLVR plateau in non-verifiable domains** — math/code RL doesn't trivially transfer.
4. **Memory architectures** — no consensus; long-context vs. RAG vs. OS-style vs. graph-structured.
5. **Multi-agent coordination** — LLM "round-tables" lack MARL's credit assignment; MARL lacks LLM-agents' generality.

# Robotics + Embodied AI + VLA + Sim2Real Survey (2024-2026)

## 1. Vision-Language-Action (VLA) Models

**RT-1 / RT-2 (Google DeepMind, 2022/2023)**
- RT-1 (Dec 2022): Transformer trained on 130k demonstrations from 13 robots over 17 months; 97% success on 700+ training instructions. RT-2 (Jul 2023, arXiv:2307.15818): co-fine-tunes PaLI-X/PaLM-E on robot + web data, enabling chain-of-thought multi-step reasoning and emergent generalization. **Limitation:** closed weights; latency too high for dexterous control; struggles with novel skills not implied by web priors.

**OpenVLA (Stanford/Berkeley/TRI/DeepMind/PI, 2024) — arXiv:2406.09246**
- 7B-parameter open-source VLA on Llama-2 + SigLIP/DINOv2, trained on 970k Open X-Embodiment trajectories. Outperforms Diffusion Policy by 20.4% on multi-task, multi-object generalization; LoRA fine-tunable on consumer GPUs; 4-bit quantization preserves performance. **Limitation:** ~5 Hz inference; discrete action tokens limit precision; single-image observation hurts depth tasks. (https://openvla.github.io)

**π0 / π0-FAST / π0.5 (Physical Intelligence, 2024-2025)**
- π0 (Oct 2024): generalist policy combining a VLM with flow-matching action expert for high-frequency (50 Hz) continuous control across 7 robot platforms. π0-FAST (Jan 2025): autoregressive variant with DCT-based action tokenizer (5x training speedup). **π0.5** (arXiv:2504.16054, Apr 2025): adds hierarchical reasoning + heterogeneous co-training (web data, verbal instructions, multi-robot data) for open-world generalization — cleans entirely novel kitchens/bedrooms. **Limitation:** Long-horizon failures, expensive demonstration data, generalization still narrow vs. truly novel objects. (https://www.physicalintelligence.company/blog/pi05)

**Figure Helix / Figure 02 / Figure 03 (Figure AI, 2025)**
- Helix (Feb 2025): dual-process VLA — S2 (7-9 Hz internet-pretrained VLM for scene/goal reasoning) + S1 (200 Hz visuomotor policy controlling 35 DoF). Runs entirely on embedded GPU. Figure 03 (Oct 2025): redesigned hardware for Helix — 2x camera frame rate, ¼ latency, 60% wider FoV, palm cameras, 3-gram tactile fingertips; designed for 12k units/yr production. **Limitation:** closed-source; benchmark numbers not peer-reviewed; reliance on extensive in-house teleop data. (https://www.figure.ai/news/introducing-figure-03)

**NVIDIA Isaac GR00T N1 (Mar 2025) — arXiv:2503.14734**
- First open humanoid foundation model. Dual-system (System 1 fast diffusion-transformer action head + System 2 NVIDIA Eagle-2 VLM). GR00T-N1-2B (2.2B params, 1.34B VLM) samples 16 actions in 63.9 ms on L40 GPU. Trained on real trajectories + human video + synthetic data (from Cosmos/Omniverse). **Limitation:** narrow real-world dexterity benchmarks; sim2real still requires domain randomization tuning. (https://research.nvidia.com/labs/lpr/publication/gr00tn1_2025/)

**Gemini Robotics / Gemini Robotics 1.5 / -ER (Google DeepMind, 2025) — arXiv:2503.20020**
- Built on Gemini 2.0; smooth reactive manipulation across diverse embodiments and unseen environments with open-vocabulary instructions. Gemini Robotics-ER (Embodied Reasoning) and 1.5 (Sep 2025) add agentic tool use (web search planning) and on-device deployment. Powers Apptronik Apollo. **Limitation:** closed model; cloud dependency for full variants; multimodal reasoning latency.

**Magma (Microsoft, CVPR 2025) — arXiv:2502.13130**
- Foundation model unifying digital UI navigation and physical robot manipulation. Introduces Set-of-Mark (SoM) and Trace-of-Mark (ToM) annotations for action grounding. SOTA on UI + robot benchmarks with single backbone. **Limitation:** zero-shot real-robot performance still lags specialist VLAs on dexterous tasks.

## 2. Diffusion Policy & Imitation Learning

**Diffusion Policy (Chi et al., RSS 2023; IJRR 2025) — arXiv:2303.04137**
- Visuomotor policy as conditional denoising diffusion; +46.9% over prior SOTA across 12 tasks / 4 benchmarks; handles multimodal action distributions, high-dim continuous spaces, training stability via receding-horizon prediction. **Limitation:** sample-inefficient at inference (multiple denoising steps); poor cross-embodiment transfer.

**3D Diffusion Policy (DP3, 2024) — arXiv:2403.03954**
- Point-cloud encoder + diffusion policy. 85% success on 4 real tasks with only 40 demos each; +24.2% over baselines in 72 sim tasks with only 10 demos. **Limitation:** requires depth sensing; struggles with reflective/transparent objects.

**DexCap (Stanford/Movement Lab, RSS 2024)**
- Portable mocap glove + chest-mounted RGB-D for in-the-wild dexterous demonstration collection; diffusion policy on point clouds. **Limitation:** embodiment mismatch between human hand demos and robot manipulator.

**Mobile ALOHA / ALOHA (Stanford, 2024)**
- $32k bimanual whole-body teleop system; ACT (Action Chunking Transformer) imitation learning. Demonstrated cooking, cleaning. **Limitation:** ~50 demos per task; brittle to novel scenes.

**Octo (Berkeley/Stanford/CMU, RSS 2024) — arXiv:2405.12213**
- Open transformer-based diffusion policy, 800k Open X-Embodiment episodes; language- or goal-conditioned; fine-tunable to new robots/sensors in hours.

**CrossFormer (Berkeley, 2024)**
- Single transformer controlling single/dual-arm manipulators, wheeled robots, quadcopters, quadrupeds — same weights, different action heads. **Limitation:** Performance still below specialist policies per embodiment.

## 3. World Models for Robotics

**DreamerV3 (Hafner et al., Nature 2025; arXiv:2301.04104)**
- Fixed-hyperparameter MBRL across 150+ tasks (continuous/discrete, 2D/3D, sparse/dense reward). First algorithm to mine diamonds in Minecraft from scratch. **Limitation:** Sample efficiency drops in extreme partial observability; world model errors compound for very long horizons.

**Genie 1 / 2 / 3 (Google DeepMind, 2024-2025)**
- Genie 1 (Feb 2024): generates playable 2D environments from unlabeled internet video. Genie 2 (Dec 2024): action-controllable 3D environments from a single prompt image (~10s memory). **Genie 3 (Aug 2025)**: 720p, 24 fps real-time interactive worlds with multi-minute consistency. Released as "Project Genie" Jan 2026. **Limitation:** finite horizon memory; physics inconsistencies; not yet a deployed RL training environment for real robots.

**Sora / Sora 2 (OpenAI, 2024-2025)**
- "Video generation models as world simulators" thesis: emergent persistence (brush strokes, bite marks) and 3D consistency. **Limitation:** fails at brittle/shatter dynamics, cause-and-effect; no explicit dynamics control.

**V-JEPA 2 (Meta, Jun 2025) — arXiv:2506.x**
- 1.2B-param self-supervised JEPA on 1M hours of video + 62 hrs robot data. 65-80% zero-shot pick-and-place in unseen environments; ~30× faster than NVIDIA Cosmos for planning. Open-source. **Limitation:** Embedding-space prediction less direct for fine control than pixel-space; sparse robot action data.

**1X World Model (1XWM, 2024-2025)**
- Video-prediction model trained on NEO robot data to enable on-demand skill synthesis from natural language. **Limitation:** generative quality not yet matching DeepMind Genie; closed.

## 4. Sim2Real Transfer

**Domain Randomization & DrEureka (NVIDIA/UPenn/UT Austin, 2024) — arXiv:2406.01967**
- DrEureka uses LLMs to jointly synthesize reward functions and domain randomization parameters. Surpasses human-tuned baselines for quadruped yoga-ball balancing. **Limitation:** still task-specific; LLM-generated rewards can exploit simulator bugs.

**NVIDIA Isaac Lab + Isaac Sim 4.x (2024-2025)**
- GPU-accelerated framework integrating physics, photorealistic rendering, multi-sensor sim. 2M env steps/s on 8× RTX Pro 6000; >900k frames/s manipulation. Now standard for humanoid RL. (https://github.com/isaac-sim/IsaacLab)

**MuJoCo MJX + MuJoCo Playground (DeepMind, 2025) — arXiv:2502.08844**
- MJX: JAX-native, differentiable rewrite of MuJoCo on GPU/TPU. Playground deployed sim2real RL to 6 robot platforms in <8 weeks.

**Newton Physics Engine (NVIDIA + Google DeepMind + Disney, GTC 2025)**
- Open-source, GPU-accelerated, differentiable physics on NVIDIA Warp; MuJoCo + Isaac Lab compatible. 70× humanoid sim speedup, 100× for in-hand manipulation. Disney BDX droids first adopter. **Limitation:** young codebase; collision/contact accuracy still improving.

**Drake (TRI/MIT)**
- Continues as the gold standard for model-based contact-rich simulation; less used for RL than for trajectory optimization / MPC.

## 5. Humanoid Robots

**Tesla Optimus Gen 3 (Oct 2025)** — kung-fu, cooking, cleaning via observation; 5k units target end-2025; $20-30k target price.
**Figure 02/03 (2024/Oct 2025)** — see Helix above.
**Unitree H1 / G1** — open RL gym (`unitree_rl_gym`); G1 is the academic workhorse for whole-body RL.
**1X NEO** — consumer humanoid, $20k early access / $499/mo subscription; deliveries 2026.
**Apptronik Apollo** — $520M raise at $5B valuation (Feb 2026); Google DeepMind partnership using Gemini Robotics; deployed at Mercedes-Benz, GXO, Jabil.
**Sanctuary AI** — Carbon AI platform; $140M+ funding.

**Berkeley Humanoid / Fast RL on G1 (2024-2025)** — Off-policy RL learns G1 locomotion in 15 min on RTX 4090 (arXiv:2512.01996). LeVERB (Berkeley + CMU): zero-shot sim-to-real whole-body control from language.

**Whole-body teleoperation stack:**
- **HumanPlus** (Stanford, 2024) — humanoid shadowing from RGB human video.
- **H2O / OmniH2O** (CMU LeCAR, CoRL 2024) — kinematic-pose universal interface; VR + verbal + RGB control; GPT-4 autonomy; releases OmniH2O-6 dataset.
- **ASAP** (CMU/NVIDIA, 2025) — sim-pretrain motion tracking then learn a delta-action residual model from real data to close dynamics gap; enables agile parkour/dance.

**Open problems:** humanoid dexterity (bimanual contact-rich), safety verification, on-device VLA latency, reliable battery-life autonomous operation (Figure recently claimed 8-hr autonomous shifts).

## 6. Manipulation Benchmarks

- **LIBERO** (UT Austin/NVIDIA, NeurIPS 2024) — dominant VLA benchmark; lifelong learning suites (Spatial/Object/Goal/Long); recent LIBERO-PRO (arXiv:2510.03827) flags memorization issues.
- **RoboCasa** (UT Austin, RSS 2024) — arXiv:2406.02523. 100 kitchen tasks (25 atomic + 75 LLM-composed); generative-AI assets (text-to-3D, text-to-image). (https://robocasa.ai/)
- **CALVIN** (Freiburg, 2022) — long-horizon language-conditioned manipulation; still the standard for hierarchical instruction following.
- **RLBench** (Imperial, 2020) — CoppeliaSim, 100 tasks; legacy comparison.
- **ManiSkill3** (UCSD SAPIEN, 2024) — arXiv:2410.00425. GPU-parallelized rendering + physics; bridges sim-to-real for generalizable embodied AI.
- **DROID** (Stanford/Berkeley, RSS 2024) — arXiv:2403.12945. 76k demos, 564 scenes, 86 tasks across multiple universities. +22 pp in-distribution / +17 pp OOD vs. OXE.
- **Open X-Embodiment** (Google + 21 institutions, 2024) — 1M+ trajectories from 22 embodiments; foundation for OpenVLA, Octo, RT-X.

## 7. RL-Trained Manipulation Policies

- **Eureka** (NVIDIA/UPenn/UT Austin, ICLR 2024) — LLM-coded reward functions outperform human experts on 80%+ of 29 tasks; +50% avg improvement; famously taught Shadow Hand pen-spinning. (https://eureka-research.github.io)
- **DrEureka** (RSS 2024) — see above; auto sim2real reward + DR.
- **SERL** (Berkeley, 2024) — arXiv:2401.16013. Real-world RL stack (RLPD off-policy + reset-free forward-backward + impedance control). Trains peg insertion, PCB assembly, cable routing in 25-50 min, ~100% success. **Limitation:** still per-task; needs task-specific reward classifier.
- **HumanPlus / ASAP / OmniH2O** — see Section 5.

## Cross-Cutting Open Problems (2024-2026)

1. **Data scarcity** — 1M trajectories (OXE) is still <1% of what LLMs train on; teleop is the bottleneck.
2. **Latency vs. capability** — large VLMs (>3B params) give reasoning but throttle reactive control; dual-system architectures (Helix, GR00T N1, Gemini Robotics 1.5) are the consensus workaround.
3. **Generalization** — π0.5 is the first to credibly demo novel-environment cleanup; broader OOD generalization remains unsolved.
4. **Sim2real** — Newton + Isaac Lab + MJX have closed the speed gap, but contact-rich dexterity and deformables still suffer; vision domain gap mitigated by photorealistic synthetic data (Cosmos, Omniverse).
5. **Evaluation** — LIBERO-PRO shows VLAs memorize benchmarks; community pivoting toward in-the-wild evaluation suites (DROID-style).
6. **Safety / reliability** — no humanoid yet has formal safety certification for unstructured home deployment.

# DRL Literature Survey: Game AI + AI for Science (2024–2026)

## A. GAME AI

### A.1 DeepMind Classics & Sequels

**AlphaGo / AlphaGo Zero / AlphaZero / MuZero**
- Papers: Silver et al., *Nature* 2016 (AlphaGo, Lee Sedol match); Silver et al., *Nature* 549, 2017 (AlphaGo Zero, tabula rasa); Silver et al., *Science* 362, 2018 (AlphaZero — chess/shogi/Go); Schrittwieser et al., *Nature* 588, 2020 (MuZero).
- Summary: Progressive removal of human priors. AlphaGo combined supervised policy + RL self-play + MCTS. AlphaGo Zero collapsed to single policy/value net trained purely from self-play, reaching superhuman in 72 hours and winning 100-0 vs AlphaGo. AlphaZero generalized to chess/shogi. MuZero further removed knowledge of *game rules* by learning a value-equivalent latent dynamics model — first system to match AlphaZero on board games AND reach SOTA across 57 Atari games with one algorithm.
- RL contribution: Self-play generates an automatic curriculum; MCTS provides a policy-improvement operator over the network's policy prior. MuZero's key innovation: learned models predict reward/value/policy rather than full state, making planning tractable in pixel domains.
- Limitations: All require massive compute; MuZero's model is opaque/non-interpretable; struggle in stochastic, partially observed environments without specialized variants.

**AlphaStar (StarCraft II)**
- Vinyals et al., *Nature* 575, 2019. Reached Grandmaster (top 0.2%) for all three races. Architecture combined imitation learning from 971k human replays, value/policy with transformer-pointer networks, and **league training** (main agents + main-exploiters + league-exploiters) to prevent strategy cycling in self-play.
- RL contribution: Multi-agent population-based RL avoids local Nash cycling; supervised initialization is essential because pure RL was too sparse-reward.
- Limitations: APM and camera restrictions debated; brittle to off-distribution human strategies; ~$10M compute.

**OpenAI Five (Dota 2)**
- Berner et al., arXiv 1912.06680, 2019. First AI to beat reigning esports world champions (OG, April 2019); 99.4% win rate vs public over 7000 games.
- Tech: Scaled PPO on 128k CPU cores + 256 GPUs for 10 months; LSTM controller; surgery technique to update architecture mid-training.
- RL contribution: Demonstrated that vanilla PPO + massive scale + self-play can solve long-horizon (~20k steps), imperfect-information, 5v5 cooperative games.
- Limitations: Restricted hero pool (17 of 117); reward shaping required; sample inefficiency — equivalent to ~180 human-years of play.

**Cicero (Diplomacy, Meta)**
- "Human-level play in the game of Diplomacy by combining language models with strategic reasoning," *Science* 378, 2022. https://www.science.org/doi/10.1126/science.ade9097
- First AI to reach human level in Diplomacy (top 10%, 2× avg human score on webDiplomacy.net). Uses a 2.7B-parameter dialogue model controlled by a planning module (piKL — modified policy iteration regularized toward human-like behavior).
- RL contribution: piKL combines RL-style equilibrium computation with KL-anchoring to human play, producing both strong AND human-interpretable negotiations. Strategic RL grounds language generation.
- Limitations: Only no-press variants studied rigorously; long horizons and ambiguous intent detection remain hard; cannot reliably detect deception.

**DeepNash (Stratego)**
- Perolat et al., *Science* 378, 2022. https://www.science.org/doi/10.1126/science.add4679
- Master-level imperfect-information game without search. Core algorithm: **Regularized Nash Dynamics (R-NaD)**, a model-free, self-play method with KL regularization toward the average policy that provably converges to ε-Nash. 84% win rate vs human experts on Gravon.
- RL contribution: First to scale Nash-equilibrium-converging RL to a 10^535 state space (vs 10^170 for Go) without explicit search. Avoids CFR's tractability ceiling.
- Limitations: Search-free design limits adaptation to opponent exploits; specialized to two-player zero-sum.

**SIMA / SIMA 2 (DeepMind)**
- SIMA Team, "Scaling Instructable Agents Across Many Simulated Worlds," arXiv 2404.10179, 2024. https://arxiv.org/abs/2404.10179
- SIMA 2 Tech Report, Dec 2025: arXiv 2512.04797. https://arxiv.org/abs/2512.04797
- SIMA 1: Generalist agent following natural-language instructions across 9 commercial 3D games via keyboard+mouse. Behavior-cloned from human demonstrations (no game-specific APIs).
- SIMA 2: Built on Gemini. Reasons about high-level goals, holds dialogue, and **self-improves** by having Gemini propose tasks/rewards in new environments. Substantially closes gap to human performance with zero-shot transfer.
- RL contribution: SIMA 2's self-play loop uses Gemini-generated rewards for RL fine-tuning — bootstrapping an LLM-judged reward signal in embodied settings.
- Limitations: Still struggles with long-horizon planning; weak fine-motor control; evaluation tied to DeepMind-curated games.

### A.2 Minecraft / Open-World

**MineDojo & MineRL**
- Fan et al., *NeurIPS 2022* (Outstanding Paper). https://arxiv.org/abs/2206.08853
- MineRL Diamond Challenge (2019-2022) — sparse-reward benchmark.
- MineDojo: Simulator + 1000s of language-prompted tasks + 730k YouTube videos / 7k wiki pages / 340k Reddit posts as internet-scale knowledge. Released MineCLIP for video-text reward learning.

**VPT (OpenAI)**
- Baker et al., "Video PreTraining," *NeurIPS 2022*. https://arxiv.org/abs/2206.11795
- Trained inverse dynamics model from 2k hours of contractor data → labeled 70k hours of YouTube → pretrained foundation policy. First AI to craft a diamond pickaxe via RL fine-tuning.
- RL contribution: Demonstrates BC pretrain + RL fine-tune scales analogously to NLP; pure RL from scratch fails on Minecraft.

**Voyager (Wang et al., NeurIPS 2023)**
- arXiv 2305.16291. First LLM-powered lifelong learning agent in Minecraft. Three components: automatic curriculum, growing skill library of executable Python, iterative prompting with self-verification. 3.1× more unique items, 15.3× faster tech-tree progress than prior SOTA.
- RL contribution: Notably, NO gradient-based RL — uses LLM-in-the-loop "in-context RL" via blackbox GPT-4 queries. Reframes "reward" as self-verification + skill-library retrieval.
- Limitations: Bound to GPT-4 cost; hallucinated code; assumes textual scene description.

**DreamerV3**
- Hafner et al., *Nature* April 2025. https://www.nature.com/articles/s41586-025-08744-2
- World-model RL with Recurrent State-Space Model + categorical latents + symlog squashing. Single hyperparameter config solves 150+ tasks (proprioceptive, visual, continuous, discrete, 2D, 3D). First algorithm to mine diamonds in Minecraft from scratch (no human data) in ~30M env steps.
- RL contribution: Demonstrates that model-based RL can be made robust to wildly different reward/observation scales, enabling true generalist deployment without per-domain tuning.
- Limitations: Wall-clock training still days; cannot exploit videos/text like VPT/Voyager.

### A.3 Open-Ended Learning

**POET / Enhanced POET (Uber)**
- Wang, Lehman, Clune, Stanley, GECCO 2019 (Best Paper); Enhanced POET, *ICML* 2020.
- Co-evolves environments and agents — environment-agent pairs spawn new challenges via CPPN encoding + PATA-EC (novelty metric) + transfer.

**XLand (DeepMind)**
- "Open-Ended Learning Leads to Generally Capable Agents," arXiv 2107.12808, 2021.
- Procedurally generates billions of multi-agent 3D tasks; population-based training over 200B steps yields zero-shot transfer to held-out tasks. XLand 2.0 added rule-conditioned generalization.
- RL contribution: Establishes that *task distribution* design (auto-curriculum) is as important as agent algorithm for generalist behavior.
- Limitations: Compute prohibitive; tasks remain simulator-bound; transfer to real world unproven.

### A.4 Recent Game AI

**Gran Turismo Sophy**
- Wurman et al., *Nature* 602, 2022. https://www.nature.com/articles/s41586-021-04357-7
- Vasco et al., *RLC* 2024 (vision-based superhuman): arXiv 2406.12563
- Original Sophy: QR-SAC (Quantile-Regression Soft Actor-Critic) trained on 1000+ PS4s; beat world-champion drivers in head-to-head.
- 2024 update: First vision-only superhuman racer (pixels + on-board telemetry, no global features).
- 2025: Champion-level GT7 agent (arXiv 2504.09021).
- Limitations: Real cars require domain randomization that current sim lacks; safety/etiquette reward shaping is ad hoc.

**AlphaProof / AlphaGeometry / AlphaGeometry 2**
- AlphaGeometry: Trinh et al., *Nature* 625, Jan 2024. https://www.nature.com/articles/s41586-023-06747-5
- AlphaProof + AlphaGeometry 2 (IMO 2024 silver, 28/42): *Nature* paper Nov 12, 2025 — https://www.nature.com/articles/s41586-025-09833-y
- AlphaGeometry 2 tech report: arXiv 2502.03544.
- AlphaProof: Lean-formalized reasoner trained via AlphaZero-style RL on millions of auto-formalized problems. Solved 3 IMO problems (incl. Q6 which only 5 humans solved).
- AlphaGeometry: Neuro-symbolic — LLM proposes auxiliary constructions, symbolic deduction engine verifies. Trained on 100M synthetic theorems with no human proofs.
- AlphaGeometry 2: 100× faster symbolic engine; solved IMO 2024 Q4 in 19 seconds.
- RL contribution: AlphaProof's RL loop searches the formal-proof tree with policy/value guidance; provides verifiable correctness signal that pure LLMs lack.
- Limitations: Combinatorics still unsolved; formalization remains bottleneck; doesn't scale to research-level math without symbolic infrastructure.

---

## B. AI FOR SCIENCE

### B.1 Protein Folding / Structure

**AlphaFold 2 / 3**
- AF2: Jumper et al., *Nature* 596, 2021.
- AF3: Abramson et al., *Nature* 630, 493-500, May 2024. https://www.nature.com/articles/s41586-024-07487-w
- AF3 unifies proteins, nucleic acids, small molecules, ions in one diffusion-based model (replacing AF2's structure module). Better than specialized docking tools on protein-ligand, protein-NA, and antibody-antigen.
- RL relevance: AF2/3 are NOT RL — they're supervised + diffusion. RL appears in *downstream* design (RFDiffusion, RL fine-tuning for binder optimization).
- Limitations: AF3 trained on PDB (biased toward stable globular proteins); no dynamics; closed-source weights initially; conformational diversity poor.

**RoseTTAFold / RoseTTAFold All-Atom (Baker lab)**
- Krishna et al., *Science* 384, 2024 (RFAA — generalized biomolecular modeling). 3-track architecture; open-source counterpart to AF.

**RFDiffusion (Baker lab)**
- Watson et al., *Nature* 620, 2023. https://www.nature.com/articles/s41586-023-06415-8
- Fine-tunes RoseTTAFold as denoising diffusion model for de novo protein backbones. Picomolar binders generated computationally; ~1 in N test candidates succeeds vs ~1 in 10,000 historically.
- RL relevance: Generation is diffusion, but downstream sequence optimization often uses RL fine-tuning of ProteinMPNN.

**ESM-3 (EvolutionaryScale, spun out from Meta)**
- Hayes et al., bioRxiv June 2024; commercial release. 98B-parameter masked language model over sequence-structure-function tokens, trained on 2.78B proteins (1.07e24 FLOPs). Generated novel GFP requiring "500M years of evolution."
- RL relevance: Reasoning-via-iterative-decoding resembles MDP-style generation; alignment uses preference fine-tuning over protein scoring functions.

### B.2 Drug / Molecular Design

**REINVENT 4 (AstraZeneca)**
- Loeffler et al., *J. Cheminformatics* 16, Feb 2024.
- Open-source generative + RL framework. Combines RNN/transformer priors with **PPO-style policy gradient** updates against multi-property reward (QED, SAS, docking score). Supports R-group, scaffold-hop, linker design.
- RL contribution: RL with shaped multi-property reward enables targeted optimization vs unconditional sampling; curriculum learning bootstraps from low-quality scoring.
- Limitations: Reward hacking (high-score molecules unsynthesizable); narrow chemical space exploration; needs orthogonal experimental validation.

**MolGAN**, GraphAF, JT-VAE remain reference baselines; 2024-2026 trend is hybrid diffusion + RL (e.g., DiffDock-RL, GeoLDM).

### B.3 Materials / Chemistry

**GNoME (DeepMind)**
- Merchant et al., *Nature* 624, Nov 2023. https://www.nature.com/articles/s41586-023-06735-9
- Graph neural network + **active learning loop** discovered 2.2M crystal candidates, 380k stable. 736 externally verified.
- RL relevance: Active-learning loop is RL-adjacent (acquisition function as policy); not pure RL.
- Limitations: April 2024 critique (Cheetham & Seshadri) found many "new" materials were rediscoveries or compositional duplicates; experimental synthesis rate still low.

**A-Lab (Berkeley + DeepMind)**
- Szymanski et al., *Nature* 624, Nov 2023. https://www.nature.com/articles/s41586-023-06734-w
- Autonomous robotic lab synthesized 41/58 GNoME-proposed compounds in 17 days using LLM-extracted recipes + thermodynamics-grounded active learning.
- Limitations: Concurrent criticism on phase-identification claims; not all 41 successes reproduced; some "novel" syntheses were known.

### B.4 Mathematics

**AlphaTensor**
- Fawzi et al., *Nature* 610, Oct 2022. https://www.nature.com/articles/s41586-022-05172-4
- AlphaZero-style tree search in tensor-decomposition space. Beat Strassen for 4×4 in F_2 (47 multiplications vs 49); 10-20% wall-clock speedups on specific GPU/TPU sizes.
- RL contribution: Recasts algorithm discovery as a single-player game with provably correct rewards.
- Limitations: Found algorithms specific to small fixed sizes; many discoveries matched within months by classical search; doesn't beat standard floating-point GEMM at scale.

**FunSearch**
- Romera-Paredes et al., *Nature* 625, Dec 2023. https://www.nature.com/articles/s41586-023-06924-6
- Evolutionary search where LLM (Codey) proposes program mutations, evaluator scores them. First LLM-driven discovery of new math (cap-set lower bound in F_3^8, online bin-packing heuristics).
- RL relevance: Evolutionary, not gradient RL — but the fitness-guided loop is RL-shaped.
- Limitations: Evaluator must give cheap, deterministic verification; doesn't help open-ended conjecture.

### B.5 Programming / Code

**AlphaCode / AlphaCode 2**
- AC1: Li et al., *Science* 378, Dec 2022. AC2: Tech report Dec 2023.
- AC1: Transformer + massive sampling + clustering filter — top 54% on Codeforces. AC2: Gemini Pro fine-tuned on 15k problems + 30M solutions; 85th percentile, solved 43% of unseen problems; 10,000× more sample-efficient than AC1.
- RL relevance: AC1 used RL (GOLD) for fine-tuning policy on execution feedback; AC2 leans more on Gemini's base + filtering.

**AlphaDev**
- Mankowitz et al., *Nature* 618, June 2023. https://www.nature.com/articles/s41586-023-06004-9
- AlphaZero applied to assembly-instruction game. Discovered sorts up to 70% faster for short sequences, merged into LLVM libc++ (first algorithmic change in a decade; runs trillions of times daily).
- RL contribution: Latency reward measured per-instruction; correctness checked via test-equivalence.
- Limitations: Effective only on short, branchless routines; doesn't generalize beyond hand-curated kernels.

### B.6 Plasma / Fusion

**TCV plasma control (DeepMind + EPFL)**
- Degrave et al., *Nature* 602, Feb 2022. https://www.nature.com/articles/s41586-021-04301-9
- MPO actor-critic trains policy in fast simulator → deployed live on TCV tokamak controlling 19 magnetic coils. First neural-net controller for plasma shape (snowflake, negative triangularity, droplets).
- Practical follow-up: Tracey et al., arXiv 2307.11546, 2023 — 65% shape accuracy improvement, 3× training speedup.

**Tearing-instability avoidance (DIII-D)**
- Seo et al., *Nature* 626, Feb 2024. https://www.nature.com/articles/s41586-024-07024-9
- RL agent learned to adjust magnetic fields in real time to prevent the leading cause of plasma disruption on DIII-D.
- RL contribution: Continuous real-time control on safety-critical hardware — RL competitive with classical PID/MPC because plasma dynamics are too high-dimensional for hand-tuned controllers.
- Limitations: Sim-to-real gap requires per-tokamak retraining; ITER scale unproven; reward includes engineered terms that may not generalize.

### B.7 Chip Design

**AlphaChip / Graph placement (Google)**
- Mirhoseini et al., *Nature* 594, June 2021. https://www.nature.com/articles/s41586-021-03544-w
- Addendum: Nature 2024 — https://www.nature.com/articles/s41586-024-08032-5
- Edge-based GNN policy + PPO; floorplans in <6h vs weeks of human work. Deployed in multiple TPU generations.
- Controversy: Markov (CACM 2024); Cheng et al. *ISPD* benchmarks could not reproduce claims; DeepMind responded with "That Chip Has Sailed" (arXiv 2411.10053). The Nature article got an addendum, no retraction, but methodological concerns remain unresolved as of 2026.
- RL contribution: Demonstrates feasibility but contested whether it beats modern simulated-annealing baselines (RePlAce, DREAMPlace).

### B.8 Autonomous Lab / Scientific Agents

**Coscientist (CMU)**
- Boiko et al., *Nature* 624, Dec 2023. https://www.nature.com/articles/s41586-023-06792-0
- GPT-4 agent with internet/doc search + code execution + ECL robotic lab. Autonomously performed Pd-catalyzed Suzuki and Sonogashira couplings.
- RL relevance: No gradient RL; in-context tool-use reasoning. Foreshadows agentic-RL pipelines.

**Sakana AI Scientist v1/v2 (2024-2025)**
- Lu et al., arXiv 2408.06292 (v1, 2024); Yamada et al., arXiv 2504.08066 (v2, April 2025).
- v2 produced first AI-authored paper to pass workshop peer review (ICLR 2025 "I Can't Believe It's Not Better"). Uses agentic tree search guided by experiment-manager agent.
- Limitations: Critical evaluation (arXiv 2502.14297) found weak literature synthesis, shallow novelty, surface-level ablations; success limited to narrow ML niches.

---

### Cross-cutting RL Themes (2024-2026)
1. **RL + foundation models is the dominant paradigm**: SIMA 2, Voyager, AI-Scientist, AlphaProof all use frozen/LLM cores with RL outer loops.
2. **Verifier-driven RL** (formal proofs, code execution, simulators) is where DRL is winning — pure preference-based RLHF/DPO is plateauing.
3. **World-model RL** (DreamerV3, MuZero) finally crossed real-world thresholds (Minecraft diamond, plasma tearing).
4. **Reproducibility crises** (AlphaChip, GNoME) signal that the field needs public benchmarks and independent verification, not just *Nature* covers.

### Sources
- [AlphaGo Zero Nature 2017](https://www.nature.com/articles/nature24270)
- [AlphaZero Science 2018](https://www.science.org/doi/10.1126/science.aar6404)
- [MuZero Nature 2020](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41586-020-03051-4)
- [AlphaStar Nature 2019](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41586-019-1724-z)
- [OpenAI Five arXiv 1912.06680](https://arxiv.org/abs/1912.06680)
- [Cicero Science 2022](https://www.science.org/doi/10.1126/science.ade9097)
- [DeepNash Science 2022 / arXiv 2206.15378](https://arxiv.org/abs/2206.15378)
- [SIMA arXiv 2404.10179](https://arxiv.org/abs/2404.10179) / [SIMA 2 arXiv 2512.04797](https://arxiv.org/abs/2512.04797)
- [Voyager arXiv 2305.16291](https://arxiv.org/abs/2305.16291)
- [MineDojo arXiv 2206.08853](https://arxiv.org/abs/2206.08853)
- [VPT arXiv 2206.11795](https://arxiv.org/abs/2206.11795)
- [DreamerV3 Nature 2025](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41586-025-08744-2)
- [XLand arXiv 2107.12808](https://arxiv.org/abs/2107.12808)
- [Gran Turismo Sophy Nature 2022](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41586-021-04357-7) / [vision-based arXiv 2406.12563](https://arxiv.org/abs/2406.12563)
- [AlphaGeometry Nature 2024](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41586-023-06747-5) / [AlphaGeometry 2 arXiv 2502.03544](https://arxiv.org/abs/2502.03544)
- [AlphaProof Nature 2025](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41586-025-09833-y)
- [AlphaFold 3 Nature 2024](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41586-024-07487-w)
- [RFDiffusion Nature 2023](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41586-023-06415-8)
- [RoseTTAFold All-Atom Science 2024](https://www.science.org/doi/10.1126/science.adl2528)
- [ESM-3 EvolutionaryScale](https://www.evolutionaryscale.ai/blog/esm3-release)
- [REINVENT 4 J. Cheminformatics 2024](https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC10882833/)
- [GNoME Nature 2023](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41586-023-06735-9)
- [A-Lab Nature 2023](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41586-023-06734-w)
- [AlphaTensor Nature 2022](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41586-022-05172-4)
- [FunSearch Nature 2023](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41586-023-06924-6)
- [AlphaCode 2 Tech Report](https://storage.googleapis.com/deepmind-media/AlphaCode2/AlphaCode2_Tech_Report.pdf)
- [AlphaDev Nature 2023](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41586-023-06004-9)
- [TCV plasma Nature 2022](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41586-021-04301-9)
